title: Scorzoneroides microcephala
intro: Scorzoneroides microcephala, also known as Wild Salsify or Mealy Sow Thistle, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and edible roots. The roots and leaves of Scorzoneroides microcephala have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing swelling, promoting wound healing, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Scorzoneroides microcephala is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions, as well as to support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Scorzoneroides microcephala is classified in the family Liliaceae, within the order Asparagales. This plant belongs to the genus Scorzoneroides, which includes several species of annual or biennial plants.
